title_en: Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani: Life and Scholarly Formation
title_ar: ابن حجر العسقلاني: حياته وتكوينه العلمي
content_en:
Ahmad ibn Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Ahmad, known as Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, was born in Cairo in 773 AH (1372 CE). His family originated from the coastal town of Asqalan (Ashkelon) in Palestine, though he himself was born and spent most of his life in Egypt. Orphaned at an early age, he was raised and educated by guardians who recognized his exceptional intellectual gifts. He began studying the Quran and Islamic sciences as a child and quickly distinguished himself through his extraordinary memory and his capacity for sustained scholarly work.

Ibn Hajar studied under dozens of the greatest scholars of his time, traveling to Egypt, the Hijaz, Yemen, and the Levant to seek out the highest authorities in each discipline. In hadith sciences he achieved a mastery that has never been equaled: he studied under scholars with the shortest chains of transmission to the Prophet, memorized tens of thousands of hadiths and their chains, and developed an unparalleled ability to evaluate the reliability of narrators and the authenticity of traditions. He was appointed chief judge (qadi al-qudat) of Egypt, a position he held several times, combining judicial service with his scholarly work.

His bibliography encompasses hundreds of works across all genres of Islamic scholarship. Fath al-Bari, his commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari, is universally recognized as the greatest work of hadith commentary ever written. Tahdhib at-Tahdhib and Taqrib at-Tahdhib are essential references in narrator criticism. Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah represents his definitive treatment of the Companions of the Prophet. Together these works established him as the supreme authority in hadith sciences.

He died in Cairo in 852 AH (1449 CE), leaving behind a scholarly legacy of unmatched depth and breadth. Ibn Hajar's prolific output — spanning hadith, biography, jurisprudence, Quranic sciences, and history — was sustained over several decades of continuous scholarly labor that his contemporaries marveled at. The combination of extraordinary memory, tireless industry, and precise analytical judgment that made his works so authoritative was recognized in his own lifetime, and the half-millennium since his death has only confirmed what his contemporaries perceived: that no scholar before or since has matched his command of the total hadith tradition.
content_id:
Ahmad ibn Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Ahmad, yang dikenal sebagai Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, lahir di Kairo pada tahun 773 H (1372 M). Keluarganya berasal dari kota pesisir Asqalan (Ashkelon) di Palestina, meskipun ia sendiri lahir dan menghabiskan sebagian besar hidupnya di Mesir. Kehilangan orang tua sejak usia dini, ia diasuh dan dididik oleh para wali yang mengenali bakat intelektualnya yang luar biasa. Ia mulai mempelajari Al-Qur'an dan ilmu-ilmu Islam sejak kecil dan dengan cepat menunjukkan kemampuan dirinya melalui daya ingat yang luar biasa serta kemampuannya untuk menjalani kerja ilmiah yang tekun.

Ibn Hajar belajar kepada puluhan ulama terbesar di zamannya, berkelana ke Mesir, Hijaz, Yaman, dan Syam untuk menimba ilmu dari para otoritas tertinggi di setiap bidang. Dalam ilmu hadits ia mencapai penguasaan yang belum pernah tertandingi: ia belajar kepada para ulama dengan sanad yang paling pendek kepada Nabi, menghafal puluhan ribu hadits beserta sanadnya, dan mengembangkan kemampuan yang tak tertandingi dalam menilai keandalan para perawi serta keshahihan riwayat. Ia diangkat sebagai hakim agung (qadi al-qudat) Mesir, jabatan yang dipegangnya beberapa kali, memadukan tugas kehakiman dengan karya ilmiahnya.

Bibliografinya mencakup ratusan karya di seluruh genre keilmuan Islam. Fath al-Bari, syarahnya atas Shahih al-Bukhari, diakui secara universal sebagai karya syarah hadits terbesar yang pernah ditulis. Tahdzib at-Tahdzib dan Taqrib at-Tahdzib merupakan referensi penting dalam kritik perawi. Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz ash-Shahabah mewakili penanganan definitifnya terhadap para Sahabat Nabi. Karya-karya ini secara bersama-sama menjadikannya otoritas tertinggi dalam ilmu-ilmu hadits.

Ia wafat di Kairo pada tahun 852 H (1449 M), meninggalkan warisan ilmiah yang tak tertandingi dalam kedalaman dan keluasannya. Produktivitas Ibn Hajar yang luar biasa — mencakup hadits, biografi, fikih, ilmu Al-Qur'an, dan sejarah — dipertahankan selama beberapa dekade kerja ilmiah yang tiada henti sehingga dikagumi oleh para ilmuwan sezamannya. Perpaduan antara daya ingat yang luar biasa, ketekunan yang tak kenal lelah, dan ketajaman analitis yang menjadikan karya-karyanya begitu otoritatif telah diakui semasa hidupnya, dan setengah milenium sejak wafatnya hanya semakin meneguhkan apa yang telah disadari oleh orang-orang sezamannya: bahwa tidak ada seorang ulama pun sebelum atau sesudahnya yang menyamai penguasaannya atas keseluruhan tradisi hadits.
title_id: Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani: Kehidupan dan Pembentukan Keilmuan
